coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +11.6% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 94 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.7× the sustained auto DPS: try Spectral Schematics over Fire Blossom (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 777 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +59% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Fire Blossom (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 93 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.8× the sustained auto DPS: try Spectral Schematics over Fire Blossom (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite, at the cost of 23% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite, at the cost of 23% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 21% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Shinbi's main threat is Line Tempo; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 21% of this kit's damage.

notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Steel (it never builds Xenia, Gaia Greaves) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 669 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 26 — about 5,565 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 6% of our best core on magical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 669 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
